NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO AUGMENT OR AMEND EXPERT WITNESS INFORMATION OR SUBMIT TARDY EXPERT WITNESS INFORMATION (TRANSACTION ID # 59803731) FILED BY DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO HEARING SET FOR NOV-16-2016 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO SUBMIT TARDY EXPERT WITNESS INFORMATION IS GRANTED. PER CCP 2034.710, BASED ON GOOD CAUSE SHOWN AND THE ABSENCE OF ANY PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFFS, THE CITY IS GRANTED LEAVE TO SUBMIT MR. SHEINFELD'S DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF THE CITY'S EXPERT WITNESS DISCLOSURE AS OF THE DATE THAT DECLARATION WAS SERVED ON MR. CASEY. BECAUSE PLAINTIFFS HAD A PLAUSIBLE ARGUMENT THAT THE CITY DID NOT MEET THE REQUIREMENTS FOR RELIEF UNDER CCP 2034.710, THE CITY'S REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: KELLEY ANN DEYOUNG, CSR #6780. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Augment Or Amend Expert Witness Information Or Submit Tardy Expert Witness Information
Off Calendar Nov-16-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Frederick Sheinfield of Office of the City Attorney telephone number 415-554-3872 for Defendant City and County of San Francisco.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion to submit tardy expert witness information is granted. Per CCP 2034.710, based on good cause shown and the absence of any prejudice to plaintiffs, the City is granted leave to submit Mr. Sheinfeld's declaration in support of the City's expert witness disclosure as of the date that declaration was served on Mr. Casey. Because plaintiffs had a plausible argument that the City did not meet the requirements for relief under CCP 2034.710, the City's request for monetary sanctions is denied. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Kelley Anne DeYoung, CSR #6780. (302/HK)

Notice And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-05-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:

Attorney, Edward C. Casey, Jr., Esq. (510.251.2300) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFFS TOMMIE COLLINS AND THORESE BRANNER'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is GRANTED. Plaintiffs have shown good cause for their request, it is supported by the liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings, and defendant is not prejudiced by the granting of the motion. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)
